name: Chocolate Fondant
recipeIngredient: 2 1-oz squares Unsweetened chocolate, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size deep saucepan, mix sugar, corn syrup, and water. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring to dissolve sugar.
2. When boiling, wash sugar crystals from the side of the pan with a pastry brush dipped in water.
3. Set candy thermometer into the pan and boil the syrup without stirring to 242°F (firm ball stage).
4. Rinse a large platter in cold water but do not dry it.
5. Pour the syrup onto the platter, but do not scrape the pan. Let stand for 5 minutes or until the surface feels just warm, moving the platter a few times to cool the surface.
6. Work the candy with a spatula or wooden spoon, scraping from the center of the platter until it is white and firm.
7. Scrape the candy into heavy or doubled plastic bags. Add melted unsweetened chocolate.
8. Close the bag and knead until the candy is well mixed, smooth, and clings together.
9. Shape into 1-inch balls. Press pecan halves into half of the balls.
10. Coat the remaining balls: Melt white chocolate in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring occasionally (do not overheat; chocolate will separate).
11. Spear the fondant balls on a fork and dip into the melted white chocolate. Tap the fork against the rim of the pan to knock off excess chocolate.
12. Place the balls on waxed paper to cool. Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place. Keeps about 1 month.
